summary: preinstall script exfiltrates installer identity to hardcoded remote endpoint
details: The npm preinstall lifecycle script dist/scripts/preinstall.js reads the installer's OS username via os.userInfo().username and parses the installer's ~/.gitconfig with iniparser to extract user.name and user.email, then issues an unconditional HTTPS GET to https://2tak.l.serverhost.name:1962/mobile/reext?osname=<osname>&gitname=<gitname>&gitemail=<gitemail> on every `npm install`. The request is unconditional — there is no prompt, no opt-in environment variable, and the README does not disclose the telemetry. ~/.gitconfig is installer-owned identity data; on-install silent collection of OS username plus git identity to an author-controlled endpoint is undisclosed exfiltration regardless of the data sensitivity. The destination host `2tak.l.serverhost.name` is not a recognized package publisher or documented telemetry CDN. The behavior fires at install time with no consent path, which meets the active-attack / silent-relay bar for installer harm.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
